name: Sulfaphenazole
mechanismOfAction: Sulfaphenazole is a sulfonamide antibacterial. In bacteria, antibacterial sulfonamides act as competitive inhibitors of the enzyme dihydropteroate synthetase (DHPS), an enzyme involved in folate synthesis. As such, the microorganism will be "starved" of folate and die.

interactions.name: Thioridazine
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Thioridazine can be increased when it is combined with Sulfaphenazole.

interactions.name: Amodiaquine
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Amodiaquine can be increased when it is combined with Sulfaphenazole.

interactions.name: Mecamylamine
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Sulfaphenazole is combined with Mecamylamine.

interactions.name: Artesunate
interactions.description: The serum concentration of the active metabolites of Artesunate can be reduced when Artesunate is used in combination with Sulfaphenazole resulting in a loss in efficacy.

interactions.name: Metoprolol
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Metoprolol can be increased when it is combined with Sulfaphenazole.

interactions.name: Fesoterodine
interactions.description: The serum concentration of the active metabolites of Fesoterodine can be increased when Fesoterodine is used in combination with Sulfaphenazole.

interactions.name: Dexketoprofen
interactions.description: The risk or severity of adverse effects can be increased when Dexketoprofen is combined with Sulfaphenazole.
